demographics.living_situation: lives with partner at a private residence; partner present during the call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I haven't slept in three days, I can't stop crying, and tonight my chest got so tight I thought I was dying. I need help — I think I need one of those mental health plans to see a psychologist, I can't keep going like this.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Had a terrifying episode tonight where the chest tightness and racing heart made me feel like I was dying; I'm exhausted and overwhelmed and want to get set up with proper support.

symptom_narrative.onset: the low mood, worry and poor sleep have been building for more than two weeks; tonight it peaked in a sudden severe episode
symptom_narrative.duration: over two weeks of symptoms; tonight's acute episode; no sleep for about three days
symptom_narrative.character: constant dread and worry, tearfulness, unable to concentrate, and tonight a sudden episode of chest tightness, racing heart, fast breathing and a feeling of doom
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: tonight felt like the worst of it — 'I thought I was dying'; usually able to function but right now completely overwhelmed
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the tightness was in my chest; everything else is 'in my head' — worry, dread, can't switch off
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: worry is there most of the day every day; the severe chest episode came on suddenly tonight and eased after a while
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: the chest tightness settled with slow breathing once I calmed down a bit; my partner being here helps
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: thinking about work and things at home makes the worry spiral; lying awake at night is the worst
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: racing heart and fast breathing during the episode, hand-wringing, feeling shaky and on edge, exhausted
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: has struggled with anxiety before; nothing this severe recently
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: can't sleep, can't concentrate, feels unable to cope with normal daily tasks right now

past_medical_history.how_managed: no regular medication for it currently

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: no regular medications for mental health at the moment

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: significant stress at work and recent conflict in the relationship; partner is supportive and present tonight

communication_style.typical_message_style: tearful, overwhelmed, some catastrophic phrasing about the physical symptoms
